comments: Information of tracklisting from Hal C F Astell's website.
Tommy is knocked out by Robert Plant's new album, Manic Nirvana, and features a further track from it.
Interview with Bruce Dickinson, ahead of his debut solo album.
The Rock War features First Born from Hereford, Quit Bitching from Toxteth and Artisan from East Yorkshire. Artisan won, with Quit Bitching second and First Born third.

Tommy mentions that there will be no Rock War the following week as he'll be broadcasting live from the Newcastle Mayfair (this was to mark the belated arrival of Radio 1 on FM throughout north-east England, a renowned hard rock and metal heartland). Before the Friday Night Connection and at the end of the show, he mentions that the two bands will be Romeo's Daughter and Thunder.

After the Rock War, there's an advert for the Nelson Mandela Tribute Concert on Easter Monday, two months after his release. That was 16 Apr 1990 (for the highly questionable handling of the event by Radio 1, see Andy Kershaw's comments in The Nation's Favourite).

Friday Night Connection from Bruce Maclean of Sheffield, who got the previous connection. Those selections were from Steve Smith from Burton-on-Trent: Manowar's March for Revenge, April Wine's Anything You Want, You Got It and Uriah Heep's July Morning.

track_listing.track: First Born - Living a Lie (Rock War act)
Quit Bitchin - Don't Stop Me (Rock War act)
Dawn Patrol - Slight of Heart (FRS session)
Dawn Patrol - Shades of Grey (FRS session)
Tora Tora - She's Good She's Bad
Dawn Patrol - Explicit Through Implication (FRS session)
Love/Hate - Blackout in the Red Room
